business.type_label: business coach / retreat facilitator (True Business model)

engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is deciding whether to trust Kat as a coach/mentor — the page leans on her 30-year comms credentials, career-pivot story, process, and six client testimonials. Sells defined expertise, so top rung of the ladder = trust.

goal.one_action: Book a discovery / retreat-alignment session with Kat

structure.archetype_note: Multi-page consultant hub fronting several offers (1:1 retreat, PR course, book) that all funnel to a Calendly discovery call; book sold via a separate Squarespace shop/cart.

mapped.observed_as: Begin with a Retreat Alignment Session
Join me under a mango tree (photo gallery)
The Antigua Retreat is designed for you
My Business Roots / I followed my heart to Antigua
What I offer to visionaries and creatives
Get in touch (mailto + Calendly, no fields)

proposed_new.name: storefront-checkout
proposed_new.evidence: Squarespace Shop + Cart selling Kat's book as a one-time purchase (commerce.cart_or_checkout=true), running alongside the Calendly booking funnel.

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full; four pages captured with clear section order, CTAs, proof counts, and pricing.

gap_findings: Multi-offer consultant hub: one site fronts three distinct offers (retreat, course, book) plus a shop/cart — not a single clean conversion; the trust engine + section list assume one funnel, not a hub routing to several.

Mixed model: trust-led booking (Calendly discovery call) coexists with a real e-commerce cart/checkout for the book — engine 'trust' + capability 'storefront-checkout' both apply to the same site, which the single-engine model can't express.
